REMARK: AS TO THE DEC-17-2024 PLAINTIFF'S EX PARTE APPLICATION TO SERVE COMPLAINT; FOR IN CAMERA REVIEW OF PLAINTIFFS CERTIFICATES OF MERIT PURSUANT TO CCP 340.1(I); THE COURT DENIES THE APPLICATION WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO FILE A REGULARLY NOTICED MOTION. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

LAW AND MOTION 302,PLAINTIFF TODD BEAUGRAND'S MOTION TO SERVE COMPLAINT; FOR IN CAMERA REVIEW OF PLAINTIFF'S CERTIFICATES OF MERIT PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 340.1(I). PLAINTIFF TODD BEAUGRAND'S MOTION TO SERVE COMPLAINT AS TO DOES 1-5 IS GRANTED AS TO ALL FIVE DOES. PER CCP 340.1(H), AFTER AN IN CAMERA REVIEW BASED SOLELY ON ATTORNEY AND MENTAL HEALTH PRACTITIONER CERTIFICATES OF MERIT AS TO DOES 1-5, THE COURT FINDS THAT THERE IS REASONABLE AND MERITORIOUS CAUSE FOR THE FILING OF THIS ACTION AGAINST EACH OF DOES 1-5. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE COURT'S RULING AND MUST DROP A HARD COURTESY COPY IN DEPT. 302'S INBOX. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/HEK)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-30-2025 Continued To Jul-22-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Serve Complaint; For In Camera Review Of Plaintiff'S Certificates Of Merit Pursuant To Ccp Section 340.1(I)
Off Calendar Aug-25-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF TODD BEAUGRAND's Motion To Serve Complaint; For In Camera Review Of Plaintiff's Certificates Of Merit Pursuant To CCP Section 340.1(I).

Plaintiff Todd Beaugrand's motion to serve complaint as to DOES 1-5 is granted as to all five DOES. Per CCP 340.1(h), after an in camera review based solely on attorney and mental health practitioner certificates of merit as to DOES 1-5, the court finds that there is reasonable and meritorious cause for the filing of this action against each of DOES 1-5.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/HEK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
